reasoning: The Serbian government formed a new cabinet under Prime Minister Djuro Macut in April 2025, following the resignation of former PM Miloš Vučević in early 2025. Despite ongoing student and opposition protests demanding a transitional government and snap elections, the state leadership has explicitly rejected these demands. In March 2025, President Vučić firmly ruled out organizing early elections within six months, and in early May, key ruling party figures reinforced this stance, warning that early polls would be disastrous. Given that a new government was just installed and authorities deliberately passed on the constitutional opportunity to call elections when the previous PM stepped down, the likelihood of them reversing course to announce a specific general election date before the July 1, 2025 deadline is extremely low.
